sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first. tiny food with serious nutrient density because seeds often overperform for their size. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters. usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next. spice-like serving sizes limit real payoff because the numbers look huge, but people do not eat 100 grams of cumin seeds. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.

closing.summary: Cumin Seeds does have some solid upside, especially tiny food with serious nutrient density and easy to sprinkle into meals. but spice-like serving sizes limit real payoff and real serving sizes are often small keep it from climbing higher. best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
